column21: [I prefer to do things with others rather than on my own.]
column22: [I prefer to do things the same way over and over again.]
column23: [If I try to imagine something, I find it very easy to create a picture in my mind.]
column24: [I frequently get so strongly absorbed in one thing that I lose sight of other things.]
column25: [I often notice small sounds when others do not.]
column26: [I usually notice car number plates or similar strings of information.]
column27: [Other people frequently tell me that what Ive said is impolite, even though I think it is polite.]
column28: [When Im reading a story, I can easily imagine what the characters might look like.]
column30: [In a social group, I can easily keep track of several different peoples conversations.]
column32: [I tend to notice details that others do not.]
column33: [I would rather go to a library than a party.]
column35: [I find myself drawn more strongly to people than to things.]
column36: [I tend to have very strong interests which I get upset about if I cant pursue.]
column38: [When I talk, it isnt always easy for others to get a word in edgeways.]
column40: [When Im reading a story, I find it difficult to work out the characters intentions.]
column42: [I find it hard to make new friends.]
column43: [I notice patterns in things all the time.]
column44: [I would rather go to the theatre than a museum.]
column45: [It does not upset me if my daily routine is disturbed.]
column46: [I frequently find that I dont know how to keep a conversation going.]
column47: [I find it easy to read between the lines when someone is talking to me.]
column48: [I usually concentrate more on the whole picture, rather than the small details.]
column49: [I am not very good at remembering phone numbers.]
column50: [I dont usually notice small changes in a situation, or a persons appearance.]
column51: [I know how to tell if someone listening to me is getting bored.]
column52: [I find it easy to do more than one thing at once.]
column53: [When I talk on the phone, Im not sure when its my turn to speak.]
column55: [I am often the last to understand the point of a joke.]
column56: [I find it easy to work out what someone is thinking or feeling just by looking at their face.]
column57: [If there is an interruption, I can switch back to what I was doing very quickly.]
column59: [People often tell me that I keep going on and on about the same thing.]
column60: [When I was young, I used to enjoy playing games involving pretending with other children.]
column61: [I like to collect information about categories of things (e.g. types of car, types of bird, types of train, types of plant, etc.).]
column62: [I find it difficult to imagine what it would be like to be someone else.]
column63: [I like to plan any activities I participate in carefully.]
column65: [I find it difficult to work out peoples intentions]
column69: [I am not very good at remembering peoples date of birth.]
column70: [I find it very easy to play games with children that involve pretending.]
